summary: Frontend testing of manage-roles page (إدارة الأدوار) for permission hierarchy feature. CRITICAL BUG FOUND: Page gets stuck in loading state when navigating to /manage-roles. The page shows 'جاري التحميل...' (Loading...) indefinitely. After page reload within authenticated session, the page loads correctly showing roles list. The permission toggle logic code review shows correct implementation.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Page stuck in perpetual loading state ('جاري التحميل...') when navigating from admin page. The loading condition checks `loading || authLoading` and `!user || !user.role` block rendering even after API calls succeed. Works after manual page reload.

integration_issues.issue: After login and navigating Administration -> Roles Management, the page never exits loading state. API calls for /roles and /permissions/available are made but component doesn't render. Page reload fixes the issue.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Debug why manage-roles.tsx stays in loading state after navigation. Check if `authLoading` from useAuth() is staying true or if `user.role` is undefined when navigating within the app vs page reload
Add console.log statements to track: authLoading, loading, user, user?.role values in manage-roles.tsx to identify exact cause
Consider adding a timeout or fallback mechanism to exit loading state
Add data-testid attributes to interactive elements for better testing
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ togglePermission function (lines 137-183) correctly implements: 1) Full permission selection adds all sub-permissions 2) Sub-permission deselection removes full permission 3) All sub-permissions selected auto-enables full permission
✓ isSubPermissionLocked function (lines 186-192) correctly checks if parent full permission is selected to lock sub-permissions
✓ FULL_PERMISSION_MAPPING (lines 38-47) correctly defines the hierarchy for all permission types
✗ Loading state logic (lines 320-327) has issue: authLoading or user.role may not be properly available when navigating within the app
✗ Missing data-testid attributes on header add button and permission checkboxes

success_rate.frontend: 30% - Major loading state bug prevents testing of permission toggle feature. Code review shows correct logic implementation.

seed_data_creation: None required - existing roles data used
context_for_next_testing_agent: CRITICAL: manage-roles.tsx page has a loading state bug. When navigating from Administration tab, the page gets stuck showing 'جاري التحميل...' indefinitely. After manual page reload (F5), it works correctly. The permission toggle logic implementation is correct according to code review. Cannot test UI interactions until loading bug is fixed. Roles API works correctly (/api/roles and /api/permissions/available return 200 with valid token).

roles_page_navigation.details: Page stuck in loading state when navigating, works after reload

roles_list_display.details: Shows all system roles with permissions count and user count

add_role_modal.details: Cannot test due to loading state bug

permission_toggle_code_logic.details: togglePermission function correctly implements: full permission auto-selects sub-permissions, sub-permission deselect removes full permission

locked_sub_permissions_code_logic.details: isSubPermissionLocked function correctly returns true when parent full permission is selected

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The manage-roles.tsx component has conditional rendering that depends on authLoading (from AuthContext) and user.role being available. When navigating within the app (not reloading), these values may not be properly synchronized, causing the loading state to persist.
reproduction_steps: 4. Scroll down and click on 'إدارة الأدوار' (Roles Management)
5. Page shows 'جاري التحميل...' and never loads
6. Press F5 to reload - page now loads correctly
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Add debugging to track authLoading, loading, and user.role values. Consider using a fallback mechanism or adjusting the loading condition logic to handle edge cases where auth state is already available but not properly detected.
